sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Fulham have shown decent form in their last 5 matches, securing 3 wins, 1 draw, and 1 loss, with a notable 2-1 victory against Tottenham. Manchester City, despite a strong season, have had mixed results recently with 3 wins and 2 losses in their last 5, including a disappointing 0-2 defeat to Bayer Leverkusen at home.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Manchester City have dominated their recent encounters with Fulham, winning all of the last 5 head-to-head matches. In the last two meetings, City secured a 3-2 win at home and a 2-0 victory away, showcasing their attacking prowess against Fulham's defense.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Manchester City, sitting 2nd with 25 points, boast a strong attack with 27 goals scored this season, led by Erling Haaland's 14 goals. Fulham, positioned 15th with 17 points, have struggled defensively, conceding 17 goals, and their attack has managed only 15 goals, indicating a challenge against City's firepower.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Fulham, Raúl Jiménez will be crucial upfront, having scored 2 goals this season, while Alex Iwobi provides creativity with 2 assists. Manchester City's Erling Haaland and Jérémy Doku, with high Sofascore ratings and impactful contributions (14 goals and 3 assists respectively), will likely pose significant threats.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Fulham's high foul count (166, 2nd in the league) could lead to disciplinary issues against City's technical players, potentially resulting in cards. Manchester City’s ability to create big chances (evident in recent matches) and their tendency for high corner counts (6/8 matches over 10.5) may exploit Fulham's defensive vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Manchester City's historical dominance over Fulham, superior attacking stats, and Sofascore's predicted 55% chance of an away win, City are favored to win. However, Fulham's recent form suggests they could make it competitive, especially at Craven Cottage, but City's quality should prevail.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Manchester City leads Fulham 3-1 at Craven Cottage. The away side has shown dominance in key areas, scoring three goals through Erling Haaland, Tijjani Reijnders, and Phil Foden. Fulham managed a late response with a header from Emile Smith Rowe just before the break, giving them a glimmer of hope. City's attacking intent has been clear, while Fulham have struggled to create meaningful chances despite holding nearly equal possession.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Manchester City has been the more clinical side, capitalizing on their opportunities and showing greater control in the final third. Their ability to win duels and aerial battles has given them an edge in maintaining pressure. Fulham, on the other hand, have been less effective in their attacking endeavors, with limited entries into dangerous areas and a lack of potency in front of goal. Defensively, they've been exposed, especially against City's dynamic forward line.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players impact
halftimeSections.text: Erling Haaland has been a standout for Manchester City, not only scoring but also contributing with an assist, proving to be a constant threat. Phil Foden and Tijjani Reijnders have also made significant impacts with their goals, showcasing City's depth in attack. For Fulham, Emile Smith Rowe's late goal was a crucial moment, providing a spark of confidence. However, players like Raúl Jiménez and Alex Iwobi have been unable to make a consistent mark on the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half largely favored Manchester City, especially in the opening stages where they dictated the tempo with sustained attacking spells. Fulham had brief moments of resurgence, particularly in the middle of the half, where they pushed forward with intent. However, City's attacking waves were more frequent and effective, culminating in multiple goals. Towards the end of the half, Fulham showed a late surge, managing to claw back some ground, but City remained in control for the majority of the period.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Manchester City has effectively utilized their attacking players to exploit spaces in Fulham's defense, often breaking through with quick transitions. Their high pressing game has disrupted Fulham’s rhythm, forcing errors in the home side’s build-up play. Fulham, while showing resilience, have struggled to penetrate City’s defensive structure consistently. Their goal came from a well-executed set-piece situation, which might be an area to focus on in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Manchester City is likely to continue their aggressive approach, aiming to extend their lead and seal the game early. Fulham will need to tighten their defensive organization and take more risks in attack if they are to mount a comeback. Given City's superior quality and momentum, they are favored to add to their tally, though Fulham’s late first-half goal could inspire a more determined performance. Expect City to maintain control unless Fulham can capitalize on any rare defensive lapses.
